name: Bande – vuelta por Santa Comba y Aquis Querquennis
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Bande feature routes that traverse varied terrain, including areas around reservoirs and historical sites. The landscape offers a mix of natural paths and routes that pass by ancient structures. Riders can expect to encounter diverse environments, from open areas to sections near water bodies. The region's trails provide opportunities for mountain biking across different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Bande The most popular mountain bike trail is Salas Reservoir Loop from Muiños, a 19.1 miles (30.7 km) trail that takes about 2 hours 32 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the Salas Reservoir. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Bande – vuelta por Santa Comba y Aquis Querquennis, a moderate 16.3 miles (26.2 km) path. This route explores historical areas including Santa Comba and Aquis Querquennis. Local mountain bikers also love the Dolmen of Casola do Foxo – Rio Salas Bridge loop from As Maus, a 17.3 miles (27.8 km) trail leading through areas with historical dolmens and river crossings, often completed in about 2 hours 22 minutes. Mountain biking around Bande is defined by reservoir-side paths, historical sites, and varied natural terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Bande are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from 3 reviews. More than 90 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Bande's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there around Bande?
A: There are 11 mountain bike trails available around Bande, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails in Bande suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Bande offers 2 easy mountain bike trails. These routes are generally shorter and have less challenging terrain, making them suitable for beginners or family outings. While specific family-friendly routes are not detailed, the easier trails provide a good starting point.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Bande?
A: Mountain bike trails in Bande traverse varied terrain, including paths around reservoirs and historical sites. You can expect a mix of natural paths and routes that pass by ancient structures, offering diverse environments from open areas to sections near water bodies.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders in Bande?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Bande features 3 difficult mountain bike trails. One notable difficult route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19879696" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rio Salas Bridge – Dolmen of Casola do Foxo loop from Igrexa de Santa Comba de Bande&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 52.3 km and involves significant elevation changes.

Q: What are some scenic mountain bike trails around Bande?
A: Many trails in Bande offer scenic views, particularly those that traverse reservoir-side paths and historical areas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993771103/salas-reservoir-loop-from-muinos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salas Reservoir Loop from Muiños&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route known for its views of the Salas Reservoir.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails that pass by historical sites in Bande?
A: Yes, Bande is rich in historical sites that can be explored by mountain bike. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993782049/bande-vuelta-por-santa-comba-y-aquis-querquennis" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bande – vuelta por Santa Comba y Aquis Querquennis&lt;/a&gt; route, for example, explores historical areas including Santa Comba and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3112491" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aquis Querquennis Roman Camp&lt;/a&gt;. Another route, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6951519" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen of Casola do Foxo – Rio Salas Bridge loop from As Maus&lt;/a&gt;, leads through areas with historical dolmens.

Q: What are the typical lengths and durations of mountain bike trails in Bande?
A: The mountain bike trails around Bande vary in length and duration. Moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993771103/salas-reservoir-loop-from-muinos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salas Reservoir Loop from Muiños&lt;/a&gt; are about 30.7 km (19.1 miles) and take around 2 hours 32 minutes. More difficult trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6951544" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muíños loop from Campamento Romano de Aquis Querquennis&lt;/a&gt;, can be up to 67.2 km (41.8 miles) and take over 6 hours to complete.

Q: Are there any loop mountain bike trails in Bande?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails in Bande are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993771103/salas-reservoir-loop-from-muinos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salas Reservoir Loop from Muiños&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6951519" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen of Casola do Foxo – Rio Salas Bridge loop from As Maus&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Bande?
A: The mountain bike routes in Bande are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from 3 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, which includes reservoir-side paths and historical sites, offering diverse experiences.

Q: What other attractions can I explore near the mountain bike trails in Bande?
A: Beyond the trails, Bande offers several interesting attractions. You can visit historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3112491" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aquis Querquennis Roman Camp&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5278288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Baths of Bande&lt;/a&gt;. Natural features include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3557474" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bico do Patelo&lt;/a&gt; natural monument and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2385913" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo Caído Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. There are also impressive structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/672021" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro Laboreiro Castle&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4042827" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Bridge of Castro Laboreiro&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: In Bande we stumbled upon a special place. Hidden in the rainy season but in full use when the As Conchas dam, which retains the Limia River, drops low enough, we come across the marvelous Bande Roman baths.

In Puerto Quintela, in the Bande Town Hall, just 8 km. from the capital of the municipality, the Roman archaeological complex Aquis Querquennis is located, in the heart of the Baixa Limia – Serra do Xurés natural park, a World Biosphere Reserve and cross-border park.
The Vía Nova or Vía XVIII, the main Roman road that linked Braga and Astorga, crosses the place.
